Source_Species.reason: The study is in Arabidopsis; plant material and accession numbers refer to Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experiments were performed in Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 was used as the wild type comparator, though gh3 lines are insertional lines from Col-0/Ler backgrounds; for core experiments Col-0 is the applied WT.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper does not explicitly name GH3.1 as 'indole-3-acetic acid-amido synthetase GH3.1'; it states group II GH3s code for acyl acid amido synthetases catalysing IAA conjugation.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state group II GH3 enzymes are group II members of the GH3 family and catalyse IAA-amino acid conjugation.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper repeatedly places group II GH3s in the IAA inactivation/conjugation pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The Results describe catalysis of IAA-aa conjugates, i.e. the IAA amino acid conjugation branch.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Group II GH3s are stated to conjugate IAA to amino acids.

Primary_Product.reason: Results state group II GH3s catalyse formation of IAA-aa conjugates.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Directly supported by statements that group II GH3s conjugate IAA to amino acids, yielding inactive IAA forms.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number appears in the paper.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper explicitly frames GH3-mediated conjugation as IAA inactivation reducing active/free IAA.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: IAA conjugation is described as an inactivation/catabolic route downstream of IAA biosynthesis.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Redundancy/major role of group II GH3s is supported, but 'major IAA inactivation module' and control-step wording are interpretive, not directly stated.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Fig. 3a text says all root-expressed group II GH3s were upregulated by NaCl; GH3.4 and GH3.9 were exceptions not detected, so GH3.1 fits this statement.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same evidence as source species; measured in Arabidopsis roots under NaCl.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper tests NaCl, sorbitol, mannitol, PEG-mediated water deficit, and soil drought in gh3oct context.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: GH3.1 is part of gh3oct, and the octuple mutant showed elevated shoot IAA and enhanced tolerance to salinity/water deficit. However this is collective rather than individual-gene evidence.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used in the study: octuple knockout, RT-qPCR, UHPLC-MS/MS metabolite profiling, root stress assays, and soil drought assay.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: IAA is the focal metabolite regulated by GH3-mediated conjugation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: IAA is explicitly described as the main auxin/auxin phytohormone.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary and Discussion state IAA controls many developmental processes and is implicated in salinity/drought adaptive responses.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: IAA and metabolites were quantified in shoots and roots.

Applied_Promoters.reason: No promoter usage is described for GH3.1; this is not a transgenic promoter study.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point is supported at the group-II/gh3oct level, and GH3.1 is one of the eight deleted genes. The statement is collective rather than GH3.1-specific.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The text states gh3.1 roots had decreased IAA in single-mutant analysis and does not report a salt-tolerant phenotype for gh3.1 alone.
